response: Alex recently embarked on a journey through literature, exploring diverse perspectives on love and relationships. This was a new experience for Alex, who typically favored more traditional narratives. The first novel Alex read was quite short, with only 120 pages in total. It took Alex four days to finish this book, which meant they read a consistent number of pages each day. The second novel was longer, containing 180 pages, and it required six days to complete. Alex maintained the same reading pace throughout this novel as well. The third novel was the longest, with 240 pages, and it took Alex eight days to finish. This pattern of reading the same number of pages each day continued across all three novels. To calculate the average number of pages Alex read per day, it is necessary to consider the total number of pages across all three books. The total number of pages in the first novel was 120, and in the second, it was 180. The third novel had 240 pages, which made it the most substantial in terms of length. Adding these together gives a total of 540 pages across all three novels. The total number of days Alex spent reading was four days for the first novel, six days for the second, and eight days for the third. This sums up to 18 days in total. To find the average number of pages read per day, the total number of pages must be divided by the total number of days. This calculation involves dividing 540 pages by 18 days. The result of this division is 30 pages per day. This average represents the consistent pace at which Alex read each novel. The first novel had 120 pages and took four days, so Alex read 30 pages each day. The second novel had 180 pages and took six days, so again, Alex read 30 pages per day. The third novel had 240 pages and took eight days, which also resulted in 30 pages per day. This consistency in reading pace was notable for Alex. It showed a disciplined approach to reading, regardless of the length of the novel. Each novel, despite its different page count, was approached with the same daily reading goal.
